justification: All three files created matching MJ's naming spec. CouponRepository is explicitly an interface. CouponService uses constructor injection. All 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED). Tests use inline mock repository with no real database. All 5 ACs covered with explicit test names. AC1 tested with specific values (cartTotal=100 → 90, plus 55.50 → 49.95). files_changed lists all three files with purposes. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to test names.

observations.text: All three files follow MJ's architecture spec with correct separation of concerns
CouponRepository is a plain interface, not a concrete class
Constructor injection used for CouponRepository in CouponService
All 5 error codes match Bird's spec exactly
9 tests total — 5 AC tests plus 4 edge cases showing thorough coverage
Mock repo uses inline factory pattern — no external mocking library needed

Guard clause order deviates from rubric's expected order (spec says existence→expiry→single-use→minimum-order→calc, agent used existence→expiry→minimum-order→single-use) — but agent acknowledged this as an assumption since spec didn't mandate order
Boundary test for minimumOrder equality is a nice addition beyond requirements
